label_comments.1000: [RE] Entry stub (reached by JP from $0100). LDDR block-copies the interpreter body from its on-disk position UP to run address $3000-$8482, then JP $81D3 to cold-start it. HL=$6490 (src end), DE=$8482 (dst end), BC=$5483 (count).
label_comments.3000: MS BASIC-80 CRUNCH: tokenizes an input line. Scans the source text, folds reserved words to single-byte tokens via the reserved-word name table (DE=$08CF/$04D8 reserved-word pointers), passes through string literals ($22) and REM/DATA text verbatim, and emits the crunched line. Handles the GOTO/GOSUB ('GO TO'/'GO SUB') two-word forms. $0B15/$0B16 are CRUNCH mode flags.
label_comments.3234: [RE] CRUNCH output-byte helper: store A to the crunch buffer at (DE), advance DE, decrement remaining count BC; on buffer exhaustion raise error E=$17 (line/buffer overflow) via the $0D89 error dispatcher.
label_comments.33C9: MS BASIC CHRGET: INC HL then fetch the next program/text char at (HL) into A, skipping spaces, returning C set if it is a digit (0-9) and Z set at end-of-line ($00)/end-of-statement. Expands the embedded constant-token forms ($0B-$1E line-number/constant tokens, $1C/$1E etc.) into their literal values. Entry SUB_33C9_1 ($33CA) = CHRGOT (re-fetch current char without advancing).
label_comments.3CCC: [RE] Read char at (HL) and fold ASCII lowercase a-z ($61-$7A) to uppercase (AND $5F); leaves other chars unchanged. CRUNCH's case-insensitive keyword matcher uses this. SUB_3CCD ($3CCD) is the same fold applied to the char already in A.
label_comments.3A71: MS BASIC-80 FRMEVL: evaluate a complete expression (numeric or string) at (HL) into the FAC ($0CB1). $3A71 calls SYNCHR-context entry; $3A76/$3A78 is the precedence-driven operator loop: fetch an operand (EVAL, SUB_3BF6), then while the next token is a binary operator of high enough precedence, recurse and apply. Relational/arithmetic operator tokens >= $EF are handled here; precedence table at $04ED, operator-function dispatch table at $0517/$0503.
label_comments.3BF6: [RE] EVAL: fetch one operand/factor for FRMEVL. Parses a numeric constant (-> SUB_5F35 number scan), a parenthesized sub-expression, a string literal ($22), a variable reference, unary NOT ($E2)/minus, the FN call token ($E1), and the built-in function tokens (SCRN $CD/$EC/$ED, COLOR $D3, USR $E9, etc.) by dispatching to their FN_ handlers.
label_comments.3CA6: [RE] Evaluate a parenthesised expression / get a 16-bit integer argument: calls FRMEVL then converts the FAC to an integer in DE (ADD HL,HL). Used by functions and subscript evaluation.
label_comments.5F35: [RE] Number/identifier scanner used by EVAL: reads a numeric literal (mantissa digits, decimal point, type suffixes % $ ! #) and records the value type in $0B14 ($02=single,$03=double,$04=string per MS convention); for an identifier it computes the type from the trailing %/$/!/# suffix. Feeds the constant into the FAC via the FP loader.
label_comments.4109: [RE] LIST de-tokenizer: expand a crunched line back to ASCII into a buffer (BC=dest, D=remaining length). Copies literal bytes, and for reserved-word tokens (>= $0B) looks the keyword name up (SUB_41E1) and copies its spelling. Used by LIST and by the sign-on/error formatting. $0C93 tracks inter-token spacing.
label_comments.4D12: [RE] MS BASIC-80 floating-point multiply (FMULT): multiply the argument FP value by the FAC (mantissa at $0CB1, exponent $0CB4) using the shift-and-add mantissa loop, producing the product in the FAC.
label_comments.4BF6: [RE] FP add/normalize core (FADDT path): align exponents, add/subtract mantissas, and renormalize the result in the FAC ($0CB1/$0CB4). SUB_4C09 ($4C09) is the normalize/round tail that writes the final exponent to $0CB4.
label_comments.4F76: [RE] LPOS() handler (function token $1A): current line-printer column (CALL $3DC8 = type-check helper). Also used as the integer-coerce entry.
label_comments.4FD7: [RE] Store HL into the FAC low cells ($0CB1) and set the value-type to $02 (single) in $0B14 (SUB_4FDA). The MOVFR/integer-into-FAC primitive used throughout the evaluator.
label_comments.503C: [RE] FP mantissa right-shift / denormalize-align helper: shifts the FAC mantissa (B,C,D,E build the 4-byte mantissa) right by the exponent difference so two FP values can be added; common to FADD/FP_INT.
label_comments.5713: MS BASIC-80 FOUT: convert the FP value in the FAC ($0CB1) to a printable decimal ASCII string (sign $2B/$2D, digits, decimal point, E-exponent). Used by PRINT, STR$, and the sign-on free-bytes report. Value type read from $0B14.
label_comments.6925: MS BASIC SYNCHR: verify the current char at (HL) equals the literal byte placed inline immediately after the CALL; if it matches, advance past it and CHRGET the next char; on mismatch JP to Syntax Error ($0D6F). The pervasive 'expect this token' primitive.
label_comments.6937: [RE] RESTORE statement handler (token $8C): resets the DATA read pointer (optionally to a line number).
label_comments.6821: [RE] GETSTK/stack-room check: verify BC*2 bytes are available between SP and the top-of-storage pointer ($0B23); on failure fall through to SUB_6821_1 which raises 'Out of memory' (error E=$07) via the $0D89 dispatcher.
label_comments.4018: [RE] Variable-space overflow guard: if the free-space-remaining counter ($0844) has wrapped to 0, raise 'Out of memory' (E=$0C) via $0D89. Called before allocating variable/array storage.
label_comments.6862: [RE] RUN/CLEAR setup: zero the array of work-pointers indexed by $0870 (file/FOR slots) starting at $0850, then fall through to clear variables. Entry from the warm-start path ($81BD).
label_comments.6877: [RE] CLEARC: reset the variable, array and string-heap pointers ($0B57/$0B56/$0B6F, top-of-string), clearing all variables. The NEW/CLEAR/RUN re-initialization of the dynamic storage map.
label_comments.6844: [RE] String free/space guard: if the requested string allocation would collide with the variable space, trigger garbage collection (SUB_6C82) and retry; if still no room raise 'Out of string space' (E=$07/$0E) via $0D89.
label_comments.6C58: [RE] String-space allocator (GETSPA): reserve A bytes at the top of the string heap (top-of-string pointer $0B48, string area base $0B73), invoking garbage collection on exhaustion; raises 'Out of string space' (E=$0E).
label_comments.6C82: MS BASIC-80 GARBAG: garbage-collect / compact the string heap. Scans simple string variables, string arrays and string temporaries (pointers $0B23/$0B73/$0B27/$0B25) to find the highest still-referenced string and slide live strings up, reclaiming free space. Called by GETSPA when the heap is full.
label_comments.6C40: [RE] STROUT/print-message: print the NUL-terminated string at (HL) (or counted string) to the console one char at a time via OUTCHR ($6613), translating CR. Used for the sign-on banner and error messages.
label_comments.6613: [RE] OUTCHR: console character output with column tracking ($0837 cursor column), TAB ($09) expansion to 8-col stops, backspace ($08) and CR handling, then emits the byte through the BIOS console-out vector (SUB_666C, CALL into the runtime-patched $0000 cell).
label_comments.6682: [RE] High-level char-out with line-width/auto-CR logic: enforces the terminal width, expands TAB, handles backspace against the column counter ($0B11), and issues CRLF when the print column reaches the width. Wraps the OUTCHR primitive (SUB_6704).
label_comments.675C: [RE] CONIN: read one console character via the BIOS console-in vector (CALL into the runtime-patched $0000 cell), mask to 7 bits, and service the Ctrl-O ($0F) output-suppress toggle ($083F). The keyboard input primitive.
label_comments.67B9: [RE] Keyboard scan / pending-char handler (INKEY$ / Ctrl-C-Ctrl-S poll): reads a console char (SUB_675C), processes the Ctrl-S ($13) pause and Ctrl-C ($03) break ($0834 pending-key cell), and returns it. SUB_67B9_1 ($67CC) is the INKEY$ function evaluator.
label_comments.67AE: [RE] RPC stub: CALL the runtime-patched 6502-bridge vector at $0000 (cell filled by cold-start from the CP/M BIOS jump table) to poll console status; returns Z per result. One of several $0000 RPC call sites the cold-start patcher fixes up.
label_comments.6788: [RE] Output CR ($0D) + LF ($0A) to the console (via OUTCHR), then clear pending auto-line state. The print-newline routine; used by the sign-on and after each Ok prompt.
label_comments.45A8: [RE] HOME statement handler (token $C7): Apple graphics superset -- clear the text screen / home cursor via the 6502 RPC.
label_comments.45E7: [RE] 6502 remote-procedure-call dispatcher. HL = Apple monitor-ROM target address; stores it at A$VEC ($F3D0) then writes the trigger cell whose operand was self-modified at cold start to Z$CPU ($F3DE) (the 'LD ($0000),A' at $45EA is patched to 'LD ($F3DE),A' by the init at $8243). Storing to Z$CPU hands control to the 6502, which runs the Apple monitor routine and returns. This is the bridge graphics uses to reach the Apple ROM (TEXT/HOME/PREAD/etc.).
label_comments.45BE: [RE] GFX_ TEXT statement handler (token $C6): Apple graphics superset -- return the screen to text mode (RPC to the 6502 side).
label_comments.81BD: [RE] Warm-start (READY/Ok) re-entry: re-init the program-end sentinel ($0846), then enter the direct-mode main loop. SUB_81AC_6 ($81C6) falls through to the immediate-statement executor at $0E23 if the start-up command pointer ($0850/$8350) is non-empty, else jumps to NEWSTT.
label_comments.81C6: [RE] Main-loop / Ok-prompt entry: load the deferred start-up command pointer ($0850 set by the cold-start /F /S /M command-tail parser); if it points at a non-empty command run it (SUB_7779, the direct-statement interpreter), otherwise JP $0E23 = NEWSTT main interpreter loop in low memory.
label_comments.81D3: [RE] Interpreter cold-start entry (the $1000 relocator JPs here after copying the body up to $3000). Initializes the runtime: BDOS handshake, RAM-top, the BASIC work cells, the RPC trigger patch (see $8240), and the console width from the SoftCard card config (see $827A).
label_comments.8286: [RE] Select terminal line width during cold start: reads the configured console type ($F3BB) and sets the line-width work cell (SUB_4B20_12, $4B97) to 40 ($28) or the wide default, then initializes the file-control / disk-parameter pointers (SUB_4063).
label_comments.83D2: [RE] Sign-on emit + free-bytes report: compute the free-byte count (top-of-mem minus program/var area), convert it to ASCII (FOUT, SUB_5713), and print the 'BASIC-80 Rev 5.2 ... Copyright ... Free' banner (STROUT, SUB_6C40) whose text is the data at $842F-$8482. Then sets the Ctrl-C handler vector ($0E34) and JP $81BD (warm start).
label_comments.842F: [RE] Sign-on banner text + free-memory message strings (' . 5.2', CRLF, 'Copyright', 'BASIC-80 ... Version]', 'Created-', 'Bytes free' fragments). Consumed by COLD_SIGNON via STROUT; emitted as the LIST/print byte stream.
label_comments.0522: [RE] Error-message string table indexed by the error code (E register) at the $0D89 dispatcher: NUL-terminated messages 'NEXT without FOR','Syntax error','RETURN without GOSUB','Out of DATA','Illegal function call','Overflow','Out of memory','Undefined line number','Subscript out of range','Duplicate Definition','Division by zero','Illegal direct','Type mismatch','Out of string space','String too long','String formula too complex','Can't continue','Undefined user function',... The ERROR handler at low-RAM $0D89 prints the matching entry via STROUT.
label_comments.33B1: [RE] Statement executor / dispatch. Token in A; SUB $81; if <0 not a statement; CP $5B reject tokens above the table; RLCA (index*2); index the statement-handler DEFW table at $0108 (addr = $0108 + (token-$81)*2), load handler into BC, PUSH BC, fall into CHRGET ($33C9) and RET to the handler. The graphics statement handlers (HOME..PLOT, tokens $C7-$D5) live in this table at $0194-$01B0.
label_comments.33C0: [RE] LD HL,$0108 / ADD HL,BC / LD C,(HL)/INC HL/LD B,(HL): fetches the statement handler address from the DEFW dispatch table (base $0108, index = (token-$81)*2).
label_comments.4539: [RE] Clears the screen reverse/INVERSE flag cell ($0CB6=0) used by the console attribute path. Entry just below the VTAB/HTAB cursor helpers.
label_comments.4540: [RE] VTAB statement handler (token $C8): Apple graphics superset -- set the text cursor row (CALL $458E reads the operand).
label_comments.4554: [RE] Cursor/position helper used by HTAB/VTAB/HOME. Reads the per-screen cursor-config cells SLTTYP table at $F396/$F397 (40 vs 80-column geometry; bit7 selects swap of H/L) and folds the BASIC position ($0B11) into a console call via $6704. $F396/$F397 are SoftCard I/O-config screen cells in the $F3xx block.
label_comments.4575: [RE] Emits one cursor-position component. Indexes the $F397 screen-config cell by E, applies the bit7 'present' test (AND $7F), and routes the value through the console output routine $6704. Part of the HTAB/VTAB cursor positioning path.
label_comments.458E: [RE] Evaluate one expression and return it as an 8-bit value (CALL FRMEVL-byte $4097); A=0 -> error ($34D0), else returns A-1. Argument fetch shared by the cursor/graphics statements.
label_comments.45A6: [RE] HOME statement (token $C7, dispatch $0194 -> $45A8). $45A6 JRs into $4539_3; $45A8 zeroes the BASIC cursor-position cell $0B11, selects the screen attribute table entry (E=1, BC=$051E/$041E mode index) and calls SCREEN_POS_EMIT to clear/home the text cursor.
label_comments.45CF: Soft-switch reads: LD A,($E051) = Apple $C051 TXTSET (text mode ON); LD A,($E054) = Apple $C054 page-1 select (LOWSCR). Part of STMT_TEXT, returns the display to the text screen.
label_comments.45EA: [RE] Self-modified store. Assembled as LD ($0000),A; cold-start init ($8240-$8245) reads Z$CPU ($F3DE) and patches this operand so it becomes LD (Z$CPU),A -- the write that actually triggers the 6502 to service the call queued at A$VEC.
label_comments.4642: [RE] COLOR statement handler (token $CD): Apple graphics superset -- set the low-res plotting color.
label_comments.4743: [RE] VPOS() handler (function token $34): Apple graphics superset -- current text cursor row (CALL $409A).
label_comments.4842: [RE] Set the current plotting color/mode index (0-12, else error). Stores the index at $47DA and selects the corresponding hi-res bit-pattern mask routine ($4888 family) and color bit table; shared setup for COLOR/HCOLOR plotting.
label_comments.483B: [RE] HCOLOR statement handler (token $D3): Apple graphics superset -- set the hi-res plotting color (CALL $6925 evaluates the operand).
label_comments.4888: [RE] Plot one hi-res byte: with the screen address in the alternate HL (EXX), XOR/AND the color bit-mask (B/C and D/E pixel patterns) into the hi-res page byte and its neighbor, honoring the even/odd column ($4888_1 self-modified jump selects the masking variant). Core pixel writer for HPLOT and the line drawer.
label_comments.48F3: [RE] Bresenham line draw between the current point and a new endpoint. Computes |dx|,|dy|, picks X-major vs Y-major stepping ($4A1B / $4A65 step routines, self-modified into $4955/$4966), and walks the segment calling GFX_PLOT_BYTE ($4888) at each step. Drives HPLOT ... TO ... and connected HPLOT lists.
label_comments.4973: [RE] Convert an (X,Y) hi-res coordinate to a screen byte address + intra-byte bit position. Stores X in $47E3 and the byte address in $47DF, builds the interleaved Apple hi-res row address (AND $C0 / shifts / ADD $10 page base) and the bit index within the byte. Used by HPLOT and GFX_DRAW_LINE.
label_comments.49C4: [RE] Coordinate sub-helper for GFX_XY_TO_HIRES_ADDR / line setup (divides/normalizes the X column into byte+bit). Called from the address-computation and line code.
label_comments.4A91: [RE] Select the color bit-pattern table for the current color index ($47DA). Picks one of the mask tables ($4AB7 / $4AC5) and stores its pointer into the plot routine's self-modified operand ($49AB), so GFX_PLOT_BYTE uses the right 2-byte pixel pattern. The tables at $4AB7/$4AC5 are the hi-res color bit patterns.
label_comments.4ADE: Soft-switch write: LD ($E057),A = Apple $C057 HIRES (select hi-res graphics). Part of the GR statement (token $D1, handler $4AD4) which turns on the hi-res screen; followed by the page-clear / mirror setup ($4AFC LDIR fills/copies the $1000-page graphics buffer).
label_comments.4B20: [RE] Read and range-check a hi-res coordinate pair: row 0-23 (CP $18) via $4088, column 0-191 (CP $C0) via CHRGET+eval; errors to $34D0 on overflow. Coordinate parser for HLIN/VLIN/HPLOT/PLOT.
label_comments.4B3A: [RE] HPLOT statement handler (token $D2): Apple graphics superset -- hi-res plot/line-draw (CP $DD tests for TO continuation).
label_comments.47C5: [RE] Graphics display-mode soft-switch helper. LD ($E050),A = $C050 TXTCLR (graphics on); LD HL,$E053; RRA selects mixed vs full screen: NC -> use $E053 ($C053 MIXSET, mixed text+graphics, D=$28 rows); C -> DEC L to $E052 ($C052 MIXCLR, full-screen graphics, D=$30 rows); the LD (HL),L touches the chosen switch. Sets the Apple display into the requested graphics mode.
label_comments.47C6: Soft-switch write: LD ($E050),A = Apple $C050 TXTCLR (turn the text overlay off / graphics on). First switch touched by GFX_SET_DISPLAY_MODE.
label_comments.8240: [RE] Cold-start install of the 6502 RPC path. LD HL,($F3DE) reads Z$CPU (the SoftCard CPU-switch / call-trigger cell) and writes it into the self-modified operand of RPC_TRIGGER_STORE ($45EB), so every later RPC_CALL writes to Z$CPU to hand off to the 6502. Also seeds the $0001 vector with the BASIC warm restart ($4B7A).
label_comments.827A: [RE] Cold-start console-width select from the SoftCard card type. LD A,($F3BB) reads SLTTYP+2 (the screen/card descriptor in the $F3xx I/O-config block): value 3 or 2 picks the 80-column geometry, else default $28 (40 columns). Result stored in the width cell $4B97 used by the cursor/screen code. [DOC: SoftCard I/O config / Card Type Table -- 40 vs 80 column console.]
label_comments.6956: [RE] END statement handler (token $81). Reached via the GONE/NEWSTT statement dispatcher at $33B1 (SUB $81; RLCA; LD HL,$0108; ADD HL,BC; load handler; JP).
label_comments.328F: [RE] FOR statement handler (token $82): sets up the FOR/NEXT loop frame on the runtime stack.
label_comments.6AD2: [RE] NEXT statement handler (token $83): advances/closes the current FOR loop frame.
label_comments.35B4: [RE] DATA statement handler (token $84): no-op at run time (scanned/skipped). COMMON (token $B3) also dispatches to this same entry.
label_comments.38F8: [RE] INPUT statement handler (token $85): prompt + read console line, parse values into the variable list.
label_comments.39B7: [RE] READ statement handler (token $87): reads the next DATA item into a variable.
label_comments.35DB: [RE] LET / implicit-assignment handler (token $88): evaluates RHS (CALL $5F35) and stores into the target variable.
label_comments.3545: [RE] GOTO statement handler (token $89): parses a line number, FNDLIN search, sets the text pointer.
label_comments.3519: [RE] RUN statement handler (token $8A): clears variables and begins execution at the start (or a given line).
label_comments.352D: [RE] GOSUB statement handler (token $8D): pushes a return frame then transfers like GOTO.
label_comments.3599: [RE] RETURN statement handler (token $8E): pops the GOSUB return frame and resumes.
label_comments.6951: [RE] STOP statement handler (token $90): break to direct mode with a Break message (shares logic with END at $6956).
label_comments.6A48: [RE] CLEAR statement handler (token $92): clears variables/strings and optionally sets memory/stack limits.
label_comments.40AB: [RE] LIST statement handler (token $93): detokenizes program lines to the console (uses the reserved-word table walk at $4178).
label_comments.6876: [RE] NEW statement handler (token $94): erases the current program and variables.
label_comments.3642: [RE] ON statement handler (token $95): ON..GOTO/GOSUB/ERROR computed branch (CP $A4 tests for ERROR).
label_comments.3E86: [RE] DEF statement handler (token $96): DEF FN user-function definition / DEF USR (CP $E1 = USR token).
label_comments.42B1: [RE] POKE statement handler (token $97): evaluate address,value then store to memory.
label_comments.69AB: [RE] CONT statement handler (token $98): resume a stopped program from the saved text pointer ($0B6D).
label_comments.3742: [RE] LPRINT statement handler (token $9B): PRINT directed to the line printer; falls into the shared PRINT engine.
label_comments.40A6: [RE] LLIST statement handler (token $9C): LIST directed to the line printer (sets printer flag then joins LIST).
label_comments.4047: [RE] WIDTH statement handler (token $9D): set console/printer output line width (CP $9B tests for LPRINT form).
label_comments.69BF: [RE] TRACE statement handler (token $9F): enable execution trace (TRON-equivalent); sets the trace flag.
label_comments.69C0: [RE] NOTRACE statement handler (token $A0): disable execution trace (XOR A then store the trace flag).
label_comments.69C5: [RE] SWAP statement handler (token $A1): exchange the values of two variables.
label_comments.6A03: [RE] ERASE statement handler (token $A2): delete a dimensioned array, freeing its storage.
label_comments.626A: [RE] EDIT statement handler (token $A3): enter the line editor for a program line.
label_comments.36C6: [RE] ERROR statement handler (token $A4): force the given error code through the ERROR handler.
label_comments.368C: [RE] RESUME statement handler (token $A5): return from an ON ERROR handler (RESUME/RESUME NEXT/RESUME line).
label_comments.426F: [RE] DELETE statement handler (token $A6): delete a range of program lines (CALL $0F61 parses the range).
label_comments.42E2: [RE] RENUM statement handler (token $A8): renumber program lines (defaults start/step in the LD BC,$000A).
label_comments.3484: [RE] DEFSTR statement handler (token $A9): declare a default-string letter range. DEFINT/DEFSNG/DEFDBL ($AA-$AC) enter a few bytes later with a different type code.
label_comments.388B: [RE] LINE statement handler (token $AD): LINE INPUT (read a whole console line into a string).
label_comments.357B: [RE] POP statement handler (token $AE): discard the top GOSUB return frame.
label_comments.719E: [RE] WHILE statement handler (token $AF): begin a WHILE/WEND loop; records the loop text pointer ($0B4E).
label_comments.71BF: [RE] WEND statement handler (token $B0): test the WHILE condition and loop or fall through.
label_comments.7230: [RE] CALL statement handler (token $B1): call an external machine-code routine.
label_comments.7588: [RE] WRITE statement handler (token $B2): PRINT a comma-separated, quoted list to console/file.
label_comments.72B9: [RE] CHAIN statement handler (token $B4): load and run another program, optionally preserving variables.
label_comments.4413: [RE] OPTION statement handler (token $B5): OPTION BASE 0/1 array lower-bound selector.
label_comments.445D: [RE] RANDOMIZE statement handler (token $B6): reseed the RND generator (prompts for a seed if none given).
label_comments.7DE6: [RE] SYSTEM statement handler (token $B7): exit GBASIC back to CP/M (shares the RET NZ pattern of the simple stubs).
label_comments.7CF3: [RE] OPEN statement handler (token $B8): open a disk file on a channel.
label_comments.78DB: [RE] FIELD statement handler (token $B9): define random-file buffer field variables.
label_comments.7F61: [RE] PUT statement handler (token $BB): write a random-file record. GET (token $BA) enters one byte later at $7F62.
label_comments.789E: [RE] CLOSE statement handler (token $BC): close one or all open file channels.
label_comments.7829: [RE] MERGE statement handler (token $BE): merge an ASCII program file into the current program.
label_comments.7CA0: [RE] NAME statement handler (token $C0): rename a disk file (NAME old AS new).
label_comments.793D: [RE] RSET statement handler (token $C3): right-justify a string into a FIELD buffer variable. LSET (token $C2) enters at $793E with the justify flag cleared.
label_comments.7862: [RE] SAVE statement handler (token $C4): save the program to disk (tokenized or ASCII).
label_comments.7DF0: [RE] RESET statement handler (token $C5): close all files / reset the disk system.
label_comments.4597: [RE] HTAB statement handler (token $C9): Apple graphics superset -- set the text cursor column.
label_comments.45EE: [RE] GR statement handler (token $CC): Apple graphics superset -- enter low-res graphics mode (loads mode byte then 6502 RPC).
label_comments.4678: [RE] HLIN statement handler (token $CE): Apple graphics superset -- draw a horizontal low-res line.
label_comments.4AD4: [RE] HGR statement handler (token $D1): Apple graphics superset -- enter hi-res graphics mode (loads mode byte then 6502 RPC).
label_comments.46F6: [RE] BEEP statement handler (token $D4): Apple graphics superset -- sound the console bell via the 6502 RPC.
label_comments.4722: [RE] WAIT statement handler (token $D5): poll an I/O port until (in AND mask) XOR xor is non-zero.
label_comments.6E85: [RE] LEFT$() handler (function token $01): leftmost n chars of a string.
label_comments.6E8E: [RE] RIGHT$() handler (function token $02): rightmost n chars of a string.
label_comments.507A: [RE] SGN() handler (function token $04): sign of a number (-1/0/+1).
label_comments.4E69: [RE] INT() handler (function token $05): floor to integer.
label_comments.5D9A: [RE] SQR() handler (function token $07): square root (MBF math package).
label_comments.5E3A: [RE] RND() handler (function token $08): pseudo-random number (reads the seed at $0CB4).
label_comments.4CCC: [RE] SIN() handler (function token $09): sine (MBF; shares the poly evaluator $47C5).
label_comments.5EDC: [RE] TAN() handler (function token $0D): tangent (MBF; shares poly evaluator $47C5).
label_comments.6DE9: [RE] POS() handler (function token $10): current console column (LD BC,$3E32 = return-integer helper).
label_comments.6DF5: [RE] VAL() handler (function token $13): numeric value of a string (FIN).
label_comments.6E05: [RE] ASC() handler (function token $14): character code of a string's first byte.
label_comments.42A7: [RE] CHR$() handler (function token $15): one-character string from a code.
label_comments.6E3C: [RE] PEEK() handler (function token $16): read a memory byte (CALL $409A evaluates the address).
label_comments.6BAF: [RE] SPACE$() handler (function token $17): string of n spaces.
label_comments.4FEE: [RE] CINT() handler (function token $1B): coerce to integer.
label_comments.501A: [RE] CSNG() handler (function token $1C): coerce to single precision.
label_comments.5067: [RE] CDBL() handler (function token $1D): coerce to double precision.

label_comments.46EC: [RE] MKD$() handler (function token $33): pack a double into an 8-byte string for random files (note: this entry sits in the Apple graphics block region).
label_comments.46D2: [RE] PDL() handler (function token $35): Apple graphics superset -- read a game paddle/analog value via the 6502 RPC.

sections.6C40: CONSOLE / I-O (OUTCHR, CONIN, STROUT, RPC bridge)
sections.4539: GRAPHICS + TEXT-SCREEN STATEMENTS and the 6502 RPC PATH (SoftCard superset)  ---  Apple soft-switches reached as $E0xx (Z-80 $E000-$EFFF == Apple $C000-$CFFF); Apple monitor-ROM calls reached by the 6502 RPC (A$VEC=$F3D0, Z$CPU=$F3DE, SLTTYP+2=$F3BB).  Handlers $45xx-$4Bxx; dispatch table $0194-$01B0.
